target.name: Unconventional AI
target.founder: Naveen Rao, Michael Carbin, Sara Achour, MeeLan Lee

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $475M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 10 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Unconventional AI is a Seed-stage semiconductor company founded by Naveen Rao, Michael Carbin, Sara Achour, and MeeLan Lee, having raised $475M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 11 peers in Chips & Semiconductors—three public (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and eight private firms including Cerebras, SambaNova Systems, PsiQuantum, Etched, and Tenstorrent.

Stage-progression methodology is the applicable triangulation approach here. Using total raised ($475M) as a proxy for current enterprise value and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $1.2B. This single-method approach generates a range of $1.0B (low) to $1.4B (high) by applying ±15% sensitivity bounds around the $1.2B median.

Peer-median analysis was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables in the narrow semiconductor frontier-tech segment—the dataset identified only one adjacent-stage peer and zero same-stage peers meeting similarity thresholds, falling short of the minimum three-point requirement for robust cross-sectional validation. The analysis therefore supports a valuation range of $1.0B–$1.4B, with $1.2B as the central estimate. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct peer validation, the reliance on a single methodological pillar, and stage-dependent volatility in pre-revenue frontier-tech capital structures.
